title: Cybele villosa
taxonomy: Cybele villosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is commonly known as the woolly Cybele or hairy Cybele. This plant is native to Mexico and Central America. The classification of Cybele villosa is further broken down into the genus Cybele and the species C. villosa. The plant is often found growing in tropical and subtropical regions.

intro: Cybele villosa, also known as Velvet Bean, is a leguminous plant native to tropical regions of Asia, Africa, and Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The bioactive constituents of Cybele villosa include L-DOPA, a precursor to the neurotransmitter dopamine, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. In traditional herbal medicine, Cybele villosa is often prepared as a decoction or tea, or used in the form of capsules or tablets, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
